title: One-Shot Bodies, Streaming, Uploads, Decompression, and Resource Limits · Automation and Data Client Systems · Code Recall
summary: A compliance analyst reconstructing a partial ingestion handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for one-shot bodies, streaming, uploads, decompression, and resource limits, producing bounded streaming body pipeline. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers bounded streaming body pipeline after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through predi…
objectives: Treat request and response bodies as owned one-shot streams with explicit read, close, replay, and cleanup rules. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Limit compressed and decoded response reads before allocation, parsing, logging, or persistence. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Stream large uploads with known ownership, backpressure, progress, cancellation, and retryability. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python One Shot Body Ownership · changed case · Review One Shot Body Ownership
